NKJV.1: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel:
NKJV.2: To know wisdom and instruction, To perceive the words of understanding,
NKJV.3: To receive the instruction of wisdom, Justice, judgment, and equity;
NKJV.4: To give prudence to the simple, To the young man knowledge and discretion—
NKJV.5: A wise man will hear and increase learning, And a man of understanding will attain wise counsel,
NKJV.6: To understand a proverb and an enigma, The words of the wise and their riddles.
NKJV.7: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge, But fools despise wisdom and instruction.
NKJV.8: My son, hear the instruction of your father, And do not forsake the law of your mother;
NKJV.9: For they will be a graceful ornament on your head, And chains about your neck.
NKJV.10: My son, if sinners entice you,  Do not consent.
NKJV.11: If they say, “Come with us, Let us lie in wait to shed blood; Let us lurk secretly for the innocent without cause;
NKJV.12: Let us swallow them alive like Sheol, And whole, like those who go down to the Pit;
NKJV.13: We shall find all kinds of precious possessions, We shall fill our houses with spoil;
NKJV.14: Cast in your lot among us, Let us all have one purse”—
NKJV.15: My son, do not walk in the way with them,  Keep your foot from their path;
NKJV.16: For their feet run to evil, And they make haste to shed blood.
NKJV.17: Surely, in vain the net is spread In the sight of any bird;
NKJV.18: But they lie in wait for their own blood, They lurk secretly for their own lives.
NKJV.19: So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain; It takes away the life of its owners.
NKJV.20: Wisdom calls aloud outside; She raises her voice in the open squares.
NKJV.21: She cries out in the chief concourses, At the openings of the gates in the city She speaks her words:
NKJV.22: “How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity? For scorners delight in their scorning, And fools hate knowledge.
NKJV.23: Turn at my rebuke; Surely I will pour out my spirit on you; I will make my words known to you.
NKJV.24: Because I have called and you refused, I have stretched out my hand and no one regarded,
NKJV.25: Because you disdained all my counsel, And would have none of my rebuke,
NKJV.26: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your terror comes,
NKJV.27: When your terror comes like a storm, And your destruction comes like a whirlwind, When distress and anguish come upon you.
NKJV.28: “Then  they will call on me, but I will not answer; They will seek me diligently, but they will not find me.
NKJV.29: Because they hated knowledge And did not choose the fear of the LORD,
NKJV.30: They would have none of my counsel And despised my every rebuke.
NKJV.31: Therefore they shall eat the fruit of their own way, And be filled to the full with their own fancies.
NKJV.32: For the turning away of the simple will slay them, And the complacency of fools will destroy them;
NKJV.33: But whoever listens to me will dwell safely, And will be secure, without fear of evil.”

ESV.1: The proverbs of Solomon, son of David, king of Israel:
ESV.2: To know wisdom and instruction, to understand words of insight,
ESV.3: to receive instruction in wise dealing, in righteousness, justice, and equity;
ESV.4: to give prudence to the simple, knowledge and discretion to the youth —
ESV.5: Let the wise hear and increase in learning, and the one who understands obtain guidance,
ESV.6: to understand a proverb and a saying, the words of the wise and their riddles.
ESV.7: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.
ESV.8: Hear, my son, your father's instruction, and forsake not your mother's teaching,
ESV.9: for they are a graceful garland for your head and pendants for your neck.
ESV.10: My son, if sinners entice you, do not consent.
ESV.11: If they say, “Come with us, let us lie in wait for blood; let us ambush the innocent without reason;
ESV.12: like Sheol let us swallow them alive, and whole, like those who go down to the pit;
ESV.13: we shall find all precious goods, we shall fill our houses with plunder;
ESV.14: throw in your lot among us; we will all have one purse” —
ESV.15: my son, do not walk in the way with them; hold back your foot from their paths,
ESV.16: for their feet run to evil, and they make haste to shed blood.
ESV.17: For in vain is a net spread in the sight of any bird,
ESV.18: but these men lie in wait for their own blood; they set an ambush for their own lives.
ESV.19: Such are the ways of everyone who is greedy for unjust gain; it takes away the life of its possessors.
ESV.20: Wisdom cries aloud in the street, in the markets she raises her voice;
ESV.21: at the head of the noisy streets she cries out; at the entrance of the city gates she speaks:
ESV.22: “How long, O simple ones, will you love being simple? How long will scoffers delight in their scoffing and fools hate knowledge?
ESV.23: If you turn at my reproof,  behold, I will pour out my spirit to you; I will make my words known to you.
ESV.24: Because I have called and you refused to listen, have stretched out my hand and no one has heeded,
ESV.25: because you have ignored all my counsel and would have none of my reproof,
ESV.26: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when terror strikes you,
ESV.27: when terror strikes you like a storm and your calamity comes like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come upon you.
ESV.28: Then they will call upon me, but I will not answer; they will seek me diligently but will not find me.
ESV.29: Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the LORD,
ESV.30: would have none of my counsel and despised all my reproof,
ESV.31: therefore they shall eat the fruit of their way, and have their fill of their own devices.
ESV.32: For the simple are killed by their turning away, and the complacency of fools destroys them;
ESV.33: but whoever listens to me will dwell secure and will be at ease, without dread of disaster.”

NASB.1: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel:
NASB.2: To know wisdom and instruction, To discern the sayings of understanding,
NASB.3: To receive instruction in wise behavior, Righteousness, justice and equity;
NASB.4: To give prudence to the naive, To the youth knowledge and discretion,
NASB.5: A wise man will hear and increase in learning, And a man of understanding will acquire wise counsel,
NASB.6: To understand a proverb and a figure, The words of the wise and their riddles.
NASB.7: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; Fools despise wisdom and instruction.
NASB.8: Hear, my son, your father's instruction And do not forsake your mother's teaching;
NASB.9: Indeed, they are a graceful wreath to your head And ornaments about your neck.
NASB.10: My son, if sinners entice you, Do not consent.
NASB.11: If they say, 'Come with us, Let us lie in wait for blood, Let us ambush the innocent without cause;
NASB.12: Let us swallow them alive like Sheol, Even whole, as those who go down to the pit;
NASB.13: We will find all [kinds] of precious wealth, We will fill our houses with spoil;
NASB.14: Throw in your lot with us, We shall all have one purse,'
NASB.15: My son, do not walk in the way with them. Keep your feet from their path,
NASB.16: For their feet run to evil And they hasten to shed blood.
NASB.17: Indeed, it is useless to spread the [baited] net In the sight of any bird;
NASB.18: But they lie in wait for their own blood; They ambush their own lives.
NASB.19: So are the ways of everyone who gains by violence; It takes away the life of its possessors.
NASB.20: Wisdom shouts in the street, She lifts her voice in the square;
NASB.21: At the head of the noisy [streets] she cries out; At the entrance of the gates in the city she utters her sayings:
NASB.22: 'How long, O naive ones, will you love being simple-minded? And scoffers delight themselves in scoffing And fools hate knowledge?
NASB.23: 'Turn to my reproof, Behold, I will pour out my spirit on you; I will make my words known to you.
NASB.24: 'Because I called and you refused, I stretched out my hand and no one paid attention;
NASB.25: And you neglected all my counsel And did not want my reproof;
NASB.26: I will also laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your dread comes,
NASB.27: When your dread comes like a storm And your calamity comes like a whirlwind, When distress and anguish come upon you.
NASB.28: 'Then they will call on me, but I will not answer; They will seek me diligently but they will not find me,
NASB.29: Because they hated knowledge And did not choose the fear of the LORD.
NASB.30: 'They would not accept my counsel, They spurned all my reproof.
NASB.31: 'So they shall eat of the fruit of their own way And be satiated with their own devices.
NASB.32: 'For the waywardness of the naive will kill them, And the complacency of fools will destroy them.
NASB.33: 'But he who listens to me shall live securely And will be at ease from the dread of evil.'

NLT.1: These are the proverbs of Solomon, David’s son, king of Israel.
NLT.2:
Their purpose is to teach people wisdom and discipline,
to help them understand the insights of the wise.
NLT.3:
Their purpose is to teach people to live disciplined and successful lives,
to help them do what is right, just, and fair.
NLT.4:
These proverbs will give insight to the simple,
knowledge and discernment to the young.
NLT.5:
Let the wise listen to these proverbs and become even wiser.
Let those with understanding receive guidance
NLT.6:
by exploring the meaning in these proverbs and parables,
the words of the wise and their riddles.
NLT.7:
Fear of the LORD is the foundation of true knowledge,
but fools despise wisdom and discipline.
NLT.8:
My child, listen when your father corrects you.
Don’t neglect your mother’s instruction.
NLT.9:
What you learn from them will crown you with grace
and be a chain of honor around your neck.
NLT.10:
My child, if sinners entice you,
turn your back on them!
NLT.11:
They may say, “Come and join us.
Let’s hide and kill someone!
Just for fun, let’s ambush the innocent!
NLT.12:
Let’s swallow them alive, like the grave ;
let’s swallow them whole, like those who go down to the pit of death.
NLT.13:
Think of the great things we’ll get!
We’ll fill our houses with all the stuff we take.
NLT.14:
Come, throw in your lot with us;
we’ll all share the loot.”
NLT.15:
My child, don’t go along with them!
Stay far away from their paths.
NLT.16:
They rush to commit evil deeds.
They hurry to commit murder.
NLT.17:
If a bird sees a trap being set,
it knows to stay away.
NLT.18:
But these people set an ambush for themselves;
they are trying to get themselves killed.
NLT.19:
Such is the fate of all who are greedy for money;
it robs them of life.
NLT.20:
Wisdom shouts in the streets.
She cries out in the public square.
NLT.21:
She calls to the crowds along the main street,
to those gathered in front of the city gate:
NLT.22:
“How long, you simpletons,
will you insist on being simpleminded?
How long will you mockers relish your mocking?
How long will you fools hate knowledge?
NLT.23:
Come and listen to my counsel.
I’ll share my heart with you
and make you wise.
NLT.24:
“I called you so often, but you wouldn’t come.
I reached out to you, but you paid no attention.
NLT.25:
You ignored my advice
and rejected the correction I offered.
NLT.26:
So I will laugh when you are in trouble!
I will mock you when disaster overtakes you —
NLT.27:
when calamity overtakes you like a storm,
when disaster engulfs you like a cyclone,
and anguish and distress overwhelm you.
NLT.28:
“When they cry for help, I will not answer.
Though they anxiously search for me, they will not find me.
NLT.29:
For they hated knowledge
and chose not to fear the LORD.
NLT.30:
They rejected my advice
and paid no attention when I corrected them.
NLT.31:
Therefore, they must eat the bitter fruit of living their own way,
choking on their own schemes.
NLT.32:
For simpletons turn away from me — to death.
Fools are destroyed by their own complacency.
NLT.33:
But all who listen to me will live in peace,
untroubled by fear of harm.”

WEB.1: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:
WEB.2: to know wisdom and instruction; to discern the words of understanding;
WEB.3: to receive instruction in wise dealing, in righteousness, justice, and equity;
WEB.4: to give prudence to the simple, knowledge and discretion to the young man:
WEB.5: that the wise man may hear, and increase in learning; that the man of understanding may attain to sound counsel:
WEB.6: to understand a proverb, and parables, the words and riddles of the wise.
WEB.7: The fear of Yahweh  is the beginning of knowledge; but the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.
WEB.8: My son, listen to your father’s instruction, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching:
WEB.9: for they will be a garland to grace your head, and chains around your neck.
WEB.10: My son, if sinners entice you, don’t consent.
WEB.11: If they say, “Come with us. Let’s lay in wait for blood. Let’s lurk secretly for the innocent without cause.
WEB.12: Let’s swallow them up alive like Sheol,  and whole, like those who go down into the pit.
WEB.13: We’ll find all valuable wealth. We’ll fill our houses with plunder.
WEB.14: You shall cast your lot among us. We’ll all have one purse.”
WEB.15: My son, don’t walk on the path with them. Keep your foot from their path,
WEB.16: for their feet run to evil. They hurry to shed blood.
WEB.17: For in vain is the net spread in the sight of any bird:
WEB.18: but these lay wait for their own blood. They lurk secretly for their own lives.
WEB.19: So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain. It takes away the life of its owners.
WEB.20: Wisdom calls aloud in the street. She utters her voice in the public squares.
WEB.21: She calls at the head of noisy places. At the entrance of the city gates, she utters her words:
WEB.22: “How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity? How long will mockers delight themselves in mockery, and fools hate knowledge?
WEB.23: Turn at my reproof. Behold,  I will pour out my spirit on you. I will make known my words to you.
WEB.24: Because I have called, and you have refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no one has paid attention;
WEB.25: but you have ignored all my counsel, and wanted none of my reproof;
WEB.26: I also will laugh at your disaster. I will mock when calamity overtakes you;
WEB.27: when calamity overtakes you like a storm, when your disaster comes on like a whirlwind; when distress and anguish come on you.
WEB.28: Then will they call on me, but I will not answer. They will seek me diligently, but they will not find me;
WEB.29: because they hated knowledge, and didn’t choose the fear of Yahweh.
WEB.30: They wanted none of my counsel. They despised all my reproof.
WEB.31: Therefore they will eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own schemes.
WEB.32: For the backsliding of the simple will kill them. The careless ease of fools will destroy them.
WEB.33: But whoever listens to me will dwell securely, and will be at ease, without fear of harm.”

and be glutted with their own schemes. ⓔ
CSB17.32:
For the apostasy of the inexperienced will kill them, ⓕ
and the complacency of fools will destroy them.
CSB17.33:
But whoever listens to me will live securely
and be undisturbed by the dread of danger.” ⓖ

AMP.1: The proverbs (truths obscurely expressed, maxims) of Solomon son of David, king of Israel:
AMP.2:
To know [skillful and godly] wisdom and instruction;
To discern and comprehend the words of understanding and insight,
AMP.3:
To receive instruction in wise behavior and the discipline of wise thoughtfulness,
Righteousness, justice, and integrity;
AMP.4:
That prudence (good judgment, astute common sense) may be given to the naive or inexperienced [who are easily misled],
And knowledge and discretion (intelligent discernment) to the youth,
AMP.5:
The wise will hear and increase their learning,
And the person of understanding will acquire wise counsel and the skill [to steer his course wisely and lead others to the truth],
AMP.6:
To understand a proverb and a figure [of speech] or an enigma with its interpretation,
And the words of the wise and their riddles [that require reflection].
AMP.7:
The [reverent] fear of the LORD [that is, worshiping Him and regarding Him as truly awesome] is the beginning and the preeminent part of knowledge [its starting point and its essence];
But arrogant fools despise [skillful and godly] wisdom and instruction and self-discipline.
AMP.8:
My son, hear the instruction of your father,
And do not reject the teaching of your mother.
AMP.9:
For they are a garland of grace on your head,
And chains and ornaments [of gold] around your neck.
AMP.10:
My son, if sinners entice you,
Do not consent.
AMP.11:
If they say, "Come with us;
Let us lie in wait to shed blood,
Let us ambush the innocent without cause;
AMP.12:
Let us swallow them alive like Sheol (the place of the dead),
Even whole, as those who go down to the pit [of death];
AMP.13:
We will find and take all kinds of precious possessions,
We will fill our houses with spoil;
AMP.14:
Throw in your lot with us [they insist];
We will all have one money bag [in common],"
AMP.15:
My son, do not walk on the road with them;
Keep your foot [far] away from their path,
AMP.16:
For their feet run to evil,
And they hurry to shed blood.
AMP.17:
Indeed, it is useless to spread the baited net
In the sight of any bird;
AMP.18:
But [when these people set a trap for others] they lie in wait for their own blood;
They set an ambush for their own lives [and rush to their destruction].
AMP.19:
So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain;
Greed takes away the lives of its possessors.
AMP.20:
Wisdom shouts in the street,
She raises her voice in the markets;
AMP.21:
She calls out at the head of the noisy streets [where large crowds gather];
At the entrance of the city gates she speaks her words:
AMP.22:
"How long, O naive ones [you who are easily misled], will you love being simple-minded and undiscerning?
How long will scoffers [who ridicule and deride] delight in scoffing,
How long will fools [who obstinately mock truth] hate knowledge?
AMP.23:
"If you will turn and pay attention to my rebuke,
Behold, I [Wisdom] will pour out my spirit on you;
I will make my words known to you.
AMP.24:
"Because I called and you refused [to answer],
I stretched out my hand and no one has paid attention [to my offer];
AMP.25:
And you treated all my counsel as nothing
And would not accept my reprimand,
AMP.26:
I also will laugh at your disaster;
I will mock when your dread and panic come,
AMP.27:
When your dread and panic come like a storm,
And your disaster comes like a whirlwind,
When anxiety and distress come upon you [as retribution].
AMP.28:
"Then they will call upon me (Wisdom), but I will not answer;
They will seek me eagerly but they will not find me,
AMP.29:
Because they hated knowledge
And did not choose the fear of the LORD [that is, obeying Him with reverence and awe-filled respect],
AMP.30:
They would not accept my counsel,
And they spurned all my rebuke.
AMP.31:
"Therefore they shall eat of the fruit of their own [wicked] way
And be satiated with [the penalty of] their own devices.
AMP.32:
"For the turning away of the naive will kill them,
And the careless ease of [self-righteous] fools will destroy them.
AMP.33:
"But whoever listens to me (Wisdom) will live securely and in confident trust
And will be at ease, without fear or dread of evil."

MSG.1:
These are the wise sayings of Solomon,
David's son, Israel's king—
MSG.2:
Written down so we'll know how to live well and right,
to understand what life means and where it's going;
MSG.3:
A manual for living,
for learning what's right and just and fair;
MSG.4:
To teach the inexperienced the ropes
and give our young people a grasp on reality.
MSG.5: There's something here also for seasoned men and women,
MSG.6:
still a thing or two for the experienced to learn—
Fresh wisdom to probe and penetrate,
the rhymes and reasons of wise men and women.
MSG.7:
Start with God—the first step in learning is bowing down to God;
only fools thumb their noses at such wisdom and learning.
MSG.8:
Pay close attention, friend, to what your father tells you;
never forget what you learned at your mother's knee.
MSG.9:
Wear their counsel like flowers in your hair,
like rings on your fingers.
MSG.10:
Dear friend, if bad companions tempt you,
don't go along with them.
MSG.11:
If they say—"Let's go out and raise some hell.
Let's beat up some old man, mug some old woman.
MSG.12:
Let's pick them clean
and get them ready for their funerals.
MSG.13:
We'll load up on top-quality loot.
We'll haul it home by the truckload.
MSG.14:
Join us for the time of your life!
With us, it's share and share alike!"—
MSG.15:
Oh, friend, don't give them a second look;
don't listen to them for a minute.
MSG.16:
They're racing to a very bad end,
hurrying to ruin everything they lay hands on.
MSG.17:
Nobody robs a bank
with everyone watching,
MSG.18:
Yet that's what these people are doing—
they're doing themselves in.
MSG.19:
When you grab all you can get, that's what happens:
the more you get, the less you are.
MSG.20:
Lady Wisdom goes out in the street and shouts.
At the town center she makes her speech.
MSG.21:
In the middle of the traffic she takes her stand.
At the busiest corner she calls out:
MSG.22:
"Simpletons! How long will you wallow in ignorance?
Cynics! How long will you feed your cynicism?
Idiots! How long will you refuse to learn?
MSG.23:
About face! I can revise your life.
Look, I'm ready to pour out my spirit on you;
I'm ready to tell you all I know.
MSG.24:
As it is, I've called, but you've turned a deaf ear;
I've reached out to you, but you've ignored me.
MSG.25:
"Since you laugh at my counsel
and make a joke of my advice,
MSG.26:
How can I take you seriously?
I'll turn the tables and joke about your troubles!
MSG.27:
What if the roof falls in,
and your whole life goes to pieces?
What if catastrophe strikes and there's nothing
to show for your life but rubble and ashes?
MSG.28:
You'll need me then. You'll call for me, but don't expect an answer.
No matter how hard you look, you won't find me.
MSG.29:
"Because you hated Knowledge
and had nothing to do with the Fear-of-God,
MSG.30:
Because you wouldn't take my advice
and brushed aside all my offers to train you,
MSG.31:
Well, you've made your bed—now lie in it;
you wanted your own way—now, how do you like it?
MSG.32:
Don't you see what happens, you simpletons, you idiots?
Carelessness kills; complacency is murder.
MSG.33:
First pay attention to me, and then relax.
Now you can take it easy—you're in good hands."

NIV.1:
Prologue: Purpose and Theme
The proverbs of Solomon son of David, king of Israel:
NIV.2:
for attaining wisdom and discipline;
for understanding words of insight;
NIV.3:
for acquiring a disciplined and prudent life,
doing what is right and just and fair;
NIV.4:
for giving prudence to the simple,
knowledge and discretion to the young—
NIV.5:
let the wise listen and add to their learning,
and let the discerning get guidance—
NIV.6:
for understanding proverbs and parables,
the sayings and riddles of the wise.
NIV.7:
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge,
but fools despise wisdom and discipline.
NIV.8:
Exhortations to Embrace Wisdom
Warning Against Enticement
Listen, my son, to your father’s instruction
and do not forsake your mother’s teaching.
NIV.9:
They will be a garland to grace your head
and a chain to adorn your neck.
NIV.10:
My son, if sinners entice you,
do not give in to them.
NIV.11:
If they say, “Come along with us;
let’s lie in wait for someone’s blood,
let’s waylay some harmless soul;
NIV.12:
let’s swallow them alive, like the grave,
and whole, like those who go down to the pit;
NIV.13:
we will get all sorts of valuable things
and fill our houses with plunder;
NIV.14:
throw in your lot with us,
and we will share a common purse”—
NIV.15:
my son, do not go along with them,
do not set foot on their paths;
NIV.16:
for their feet rush into sin,
they are swift to shed blood.
NIV.17:
How useless to spread a net
in full view of all the birds!
NIV.18:
These men lie in wait for their own blood;
they waylay only themselves!
NIV.19:
Such is the end of all who go after ill-gotten gain;
it takes away the lives of those who get it.
NIV.20:
Warning Against Rejecting Wisdom
Wisdom calls aloud in the street,
she raises her voice in the public squares;
NIV.21:
at the head of the noisy streets she cries out,
in the gateways of the city she makes her speech:
NIV.22:
“How long will you simple ones love your simple ways?
How long will mockers delight in mockery
and fools hate knowledge?
NIV.23:
If you had responded to my rebuke,
I would have poured out my heart to you
and made my thoughts known to you.
NIV.24:
But since you rejected me when I called
and no one gave heed when I stretched out my hand,
NIV.25:
since you ignored all my advice
and would not accept my rebuke,
NIV.26:
I in turn will laugh at your disaster;
I will mock when calamity overtakes you—
NIV.27:
when calamity overtakes you like a storm,
when disaster sweeps over you like a whirlwind,
when distress and trouble overwhelm you.
NIV.28:
“Then they will call to me but I will not answer;
they will look for me but will not find me.
NIV.29:
Since they hated knowledge
and did not choose to fear the Lord,
NIV.30:
since they would not accept my advice
and spurned my rebuke,
NIV.31:
they will eat the fruit of their ways
and be filled with the fruit of their schemes.
NIV.32:
For the waywardness of the simple will kill them,
and the complacency of fools will destroy them;
NIV.33:
but whoever listens to me will live in safety
and be at ease, without fear of harm.”

NRSVCE.1: The proverbs of Solomon son of David, king of Israel:
NRSVCE.2:
For learning about wisdom and instruction,
for understanding words of insight,
NRSVCE.3:
for gaining instruction in wise dealing,
righteousness, justice, and equity;
NRSVCE.4:
to teach shrewdness to the simple,
knowledge and prudence to the young —
NRSVCE.5:
let the wise also hear and gain in learning,
and the discerning acquire skill,
NRSVCE.6:
to understand a proverb and a figure,
the words of the wise and their riddles.
NRSVCE.7:
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge;
fools despise wisdom and instruction.
NRSVCE.8:
Hear, my child, your father’s instruction,
and do not reject your mother’s teaching;
NRSVCE.9:
for they are a fair garland for your head,
and pendants for your neck.
NRSVCE.10:
My child, if sinners entice you,
do not consent.
NRSVCE.11:
If they say, “Come with us, let us lie in wait for blood;
let us wantonly ambush the innocent;
NRSVCE.12:
like Sheol let us swallow them alive
and whole, like those who go down to the Pit.
NRSVCE.13:
We shall find all kinds of costly things;
we shall fill our houses with booty.
NRSVCE.14:
Throw in your lot among us;
we will all have one purse” —
NRSVCE.15:
my child, do not walk in their way,
keep your foot from their paths;
NRSVCE.16:
for their feet run to evil,
and they hurry to shed blood.
NRSVCE.17:
For in vain is the net baited
while the bird is looking on;
NRSVCE.18:
yet they lie in wait — to kill themselves!
and set an ambush — for their own lives!
NRSVCE.19:
Such is the end of all who are greedy for gain;
it takes away the life of its possessors.
NRSVCE.20:
Wisdom cries out in the street;
in the squares she raises her voice.
NRSVCE.21:
At the busiest corner she cries out;
at the entrance of the city gates she speaks:
NRSVCE.22:
“How long, O simple ones, will you love being simple?
How long will scoffers delight in their scoffing
and fools hate knowledge?
NRSVCE.23:
Give heed to my reproof;
I will pour out my thoughts to you;
I will make my words known to you.
NRSVCE.24:
Because I have called and you refused,
have stretched out my hand and no one heeded,
NRSVCE.25:
and because you have ignored all my counsel
and would have none of my reproof,
NRSVCE.26:
I also will laugh at your calamity;
I will mock when panic strikes you,
NRSVCE.27:
when panic strikes you like a storm,
and your calamity comes like a whirlwind,
when distress and anguish come upon you.
NRSVCE.28:
Then they will call upon me, but I will not answer;
they will seek me diligently, but will not find me.
NRSVCE.29:
Because they hated knowledge
and did not choose the fear of the LORD,
NRSVCE.30:
would have none of my counsel,
and despised all my reproof,
NRSVCE.31:
therefore they shall eat the fruit of their way
and be sated with their own devices.
NRSVCE.32:
For waywardness kills the simple,
and the complacency of fools destroys them;
NRSVCE.33:
but those who listen to me will be secure
and will live at ease, without dread of disaster.”

NET.1: The Proverbs of Solomon son of David, king of Israel:
NET.2: To learn wisdom and moral instruction, and to discern wise counsel.
NET.3: To receive moral instruction in skillful living, in righteousness, justice, and equity.
NET.4: To impart shrewdness to the morally naive, and a discerning plan to the young person.
NET.5: (Let the wise also hear and gain instruction, and let the discerning acquire guidance! )
NET.6: To discern the meaning of a proverb and a parable, the sayings of the wise and their riddles.
NET.7: Fearing the LORD is the beginning of moral knowledge, but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
NET.8: Listen, my child, to the instruction from your father, and do not forsake the teaching from your mother.
NET.9: For they will be like an elegant garland on your head, and like pendants around your neck.
NET.10: My child, if sinners try to entice you, do not consent!
NET.11: If they say, “Come with us! We will lie in wait to shed blood; we will ambush an innocent person capriciously.
NET.12: We will swallow them alive like Sheol, those full of vigor like those going down to the Pit.
NET.13: We will seize all kinds of precious wealth; we will fill our houses with plunder.
NET.14: Join with us! We will all share equally in what we steal.”
NET.15: My child, do not go down their way, withhold yourself from their path;
NET.16: for they are eager to inflict harm, and they hasten to shed blood.
NET.17: Surely it is futile to spread a net in plain sight of any bird,
NET.18: but these men lie in wait for their own blood, they ambush their own lives!
NET.19: Such are the ways of all who gain profit unjustly; it takes away the life of those who obtain it!
NET.20: Wisdom calls out in the street, she shouts loudly in the plazas;
NET.21: at the head of the noisy streets she calls, in the entrances of the gates in the city she utters her words:
NET.22: “How long will you simpletons love naiveté? How long will mockers delight in mockery and fools hate knowledge?
NET.23: If only you will respond to my rebuke, then I will pour out my thoughts to you and I will make my words known to you.
NET.24: However, because I called but you refused to listen, because I stretched out my hand but no one paid attention,
NET.25: because you neglected all my advice, and did not comply with my rebuke,
NET.26: so I myself will laugh when disaster strikes you, I will mock when what you dread comes,
NET.27: when what you dread comes like a whirlwind, and disaster strikes you like a devastating storm, when distressing trouble comes on you.
NET.28: Then they will call to me, but I will not answer; they will diligently seek me, but they will not find me.
NET.29: Because they hated moral knowledge, and did not choose to fear the LORD,
NET.30: they did not comply with my advice, they spurned all my rebuke.
NET.31: Therefore they will eat from the fruit of their way, and they will be stuffed full of their own counsel.
NET.32: For the waywardness of the simpletons will kill them, and the careless ease of fools will destroy them.
NET.33: But the one who listens to me will live in security, and will be at ease from the dread of harm.
